name: Citric Acid
description: A weak tricarboxylic organic acid used as a chelating descaler, pH adjuster, and preservative, offering superior limescale removal compared to vinegar with no odor.
appearance: White crystalline powder or granules; odorless
solubility: 592 g/L in water at 20°C; soluble in ethanol
chemistry: Chelating acid — binds metal ions in deposits, dissolving them. More effective than vinegar for descaling. Prevents yellowing by chelating iron in water.
warnings: Don't mix with baking soda/washing soda (neutralize). Not on stone. Not with bleach.
shelfLife: 3–5 years; essentially indefinite in sealed container
